perturbation.original_text: The Parties will be updating their respective business systems over the next six months, and the updates to these business systems will require the Parties to modify the Current SOPs. Once the Parties have completed the updates to the business systems and agreed on the necessary modifications to the Current SOPs, the Parties will sign a written amendment to this Agreement appending the updated standard operating procedures (the "Updated SOPs"). Until the Parties have signed a written amendment appending the Updated SOPs, the parties will continue to follow the Current SOPs.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will be updating their respective business systems over the next six months, and the updates to these business systems will require the Parties to modify the Current SOPs. Once the Parties have completed the updates to the business systems, the Updated SOPs will automatically come into effect, superseding the Current SOPs, regardless of whether a written amendment to this Agreement appending the updated standard operating procedures (the "Updated SOPs") is signed. Until the Parties have signed a written amendment appending the Updated SOPs, the parties will continue to follow the Current SOPs.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 3. While it initially states that the Current SOPs will be followed until a written amendment is signed, the added text states that the Updated SOPs automatically come into effect once the updates to the business systems are completed, irrespective of a signed amendment. This creates uncertainty about which SOPs are in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standard Operating Procedures. Over approximately the past eight years, the Parties have been supplying select Products to one another for use in the operation of their respective businesses within the United States of America, Canada and Mexico. The Parties developed and been following certain standard operating procedures in connecting with, among other topics, forecasting, production planning, ordering, delivering and resolving claims on the Products supplied to one another (the "Current SOPs"). The Parties will be updating their respective business systems over the next six months, and the updates to these business systems will require the Parties to modify the Current SOPs. Once the Parties have completed the updates to the business systems, the Updated SOPs will automatically come into effect, superseding the Current SOPs, regardless of whether a written amendment to this Agreement appending the updated standard operating procedures (the "Updated SOPs") is signed. Until the Parties have signed a written amendment appending the Updated SOPs, the parties will continue to follow the Current SOPs. The Parties will comply with the applicable SOPs in connection with the purchase and sale of products identified in a Purchase Schedule. The Parties may add terms and conditions to, and amend the terms and conditions of, the SOP in a Purchase Schedule, but any additional and amended terms and conditions in a Purchase Schedule supplementing and modifying the SOP will only apply the specific products identified in that Purchase Schedule for its duration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Initially, the clause states that the 'Current SOPs' remain in effect until a written amendment is signed. However, the added text dictates that the 'Updated SOPs' automatically take effect upon completion of the business system updates, regardless of whether a written amendment is signed. This directly contradicts the prior statement about the 'Current SOPs' remaining in force until a signed amendment, creating uncertainty about which SOPs are applicable. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'Until the Parties have signed a written amendment appending the Updated SOPs, the parties will continue to follow the Current SOPs,' directly conflicting with the new provision that the Updated SOPs come into effect automatically.

perturbation.original_text: A Party will not suspend performance under or terminate this Agreement or any accepted purchase order for a product being purchased and sold under this Agreement unless: (1) the other Party is in material breach of this Agreement and has either refused to cure the material breach or has failed to cure the material breach within thirty (30) day of its receipt of written notice of the failure; and (2) the Parties have been unable to resolve the Dispute related to the material breach through negotiation or mediation, or the breaching Party has refused or failed to attempt to resolve the Dispute through negotiation or mediation, as provided in this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: A Party may suspend performance under or terminate this Agreement or any accepted purchase order for a product being purchased and sold under this Agreement immediately upon discovering any breach, regardless of materiality or attempts at resolution. A Party will not suspend performance under or terminate this Agreement or any accepted purchase order for a product being purchased and sold under this Agreement unless: (1) the other Party is in material breach of this Agreement and has either refused to cure the material breach or has failed to cure the material breach within thirty (30) day of its receipt of written notice of the failure; and (2) the Parties have been unable to resolve the Dispute related to the material breach through negotiation or mediation, or the breaching Party has refused or failed to attempt to resolve the Dispute through negotiation or mediation, as provided in this Section.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in section 11(d) states specific conditions that must be met before a party can suspend performance or terminate the agreement. The injected text states that any breach, regardless of materiality, allows for immediate suspension or termination, which directly contradicts the detailed requirements in the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Except as expressly limited in the preceding Subsections and the other provisions in this Agreement, a Party may immediately exercise any rights and remedies available to the Party under Applicable Law upon a breach of this Agreement by the other Party. A Party may suspend performance under or terminate this Agreement or any accepted purchase order for a product being purchased and sold under this Agreement immediately upon discovering any breach, regardless of materiality or attempts at resolution. A Party will not suspend performance under or terminate this Agreement or any accepted purchase order for a product being purchased and sold under this Agreement unless: (1) the other Party is in material breach of this Agreement and has either refused to cure the material breach or has failed to cure the material breach within thirty (30) day of its receipt of written notice of the failure; and (2) the Parties have been unable to resolve the Dispute related to the material breach through negotiation or mediation, or the breaching Party has refused or failed to attempt to resolve the Dispute through negotiation or mediation, as provided in this Section. Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Party may suspend performance or terminate this Agreement or any accepted purchase order for a product being purchase and sold under this Agreement immediately on written notice to the other Party, and without providing the other Party an opportunity to cure the material breach or attempting to resolve a Dispute over the material breach by negotiation or mediation as provided in this Section, for a material breach by the other Party involving substantial harm to the reputation, goodwill and business of the non-breaching Party that cannot reasonably be avoided or fully redressed by providing the other Party an opportunity to cure the material breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The injected text permits immediate suspension or termination for *any* breach, regardless of materiality. However, the subsequent (original) text mandates a 30-day cure period for *material* breaches and requires attempts at dispute resolution before suspension/termination. This contradiction causes uncertainty regarding the conditions under which a party can suspend performance or terminate the agreement, creating a structural flaw and making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The failure of either party to insist in any one or more instances upon strict performance of any of the provisions of this Agreement or to take advantage of any of its rights shall not operate as a continuing waiver of such rights. No right or obligation under this Agreement will be considered to have been waived by a Party unless such waiver is in writing and is signed by an officer of the waiving Party and delivered to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The failure of either party to insist in any one or more instances upon strict performance of any of the provisions of this Agreement or to take advantage of any of its rights shall operate as a continuing waiver of such rights. No right or obligation under this Agreement will be considered to have been waived by a Party unless such waiver is in writing and is signed by an officer of the waiving Party and delivered to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that a failure to enforce a provision does not constitute a continuing waiver, and a waiver must be in writing. The injected text changes the first sentence to say that failure *does* operate as a continuing waiver. This directly contradicts the second sentence stating that a waiver must be explicit and in writing, creating ambiguity as to how waivers are handled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Waiver. The failure of either party to insist in any one or more instances upon strict performance of any of the provisions of this Agreement or to take advantage of any of its rights shall operate as a continuing waiver of such rights. No right or obligation under this Agreement will be considered to have been waived by a Party unless such waiver is in writing and is signed by an officer of the waiving Party and delivered to the other Party. No consent to or waiver of a breach by either Party will constitute a consent to, waiver of, or excuse for any other, different, or subsequent breach by such Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that failure to enforce a provision does not constitute a continuing waiver unless in writing. The changed text states that failure to enforce a provision DOES operate as a continuing waiver. This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in both the changed and contradicted sections that waivers require a written agreement, thus introducing a strong in-text contradiction as outlined in the explanation. The change violates the original agreement, creating ambiguity regarding the waiver process.
